$schema: see scripts/_extract_glossary_candidates.py for how candidates were seeded
_format: Each entry: term (canonical name), aliases (alt spellings/abbreviations for search), category (used for grouping/badges), certs (which exams cover it), definition (short, AWS-official-style)

entries.definition: A fully managed service for creating, publishing, maintaining, monitoring, and securing REST, HTTP, and WebSocket APIs at any scale. Supports request validation, transformation via mapping templates, custom and Lambda authorizers, throttling, usage plans, and API keys.

A fully managed integration service for securely transferring data between SaaS applications (Salesforce, ServiceNow, Slack, etc.) and AWS services like Amazon S3 and Amazon Redshift, without writing custom code.
A fully managed non-persistent application and desktop streaming service that delivers desktop applications to any computer through a web browser.
An interactive serverless query service that makes it easy to analyse data directly in Amazon S3 using standard SQL. No infrastructure to manage; you pay only for the queries that you run.

A MySQL- and PostgreSQL-compatible relational database built for the cloud, providing up to 5x throughput of standard MySQL and 3x of PostgreSQL. Storage auto-scales up to 128 TB; supports Aurora Serverless and Global Database.

A fully managed service that offers a choice of high-performing foundation models from leading AI companies through a single API, with capabilities to build generative-AI applications using your own data via retrieval-augmented generation (RAG).
A communications service that lets users meet, chat, and place business calls inside and outside your organization, all using a single application.

A fast content delivery network (CDN) that securely delivers data, videos, applications, and APIs to customers globally with low latency. Integrates with AWS Shield, WAF, ACM, and S3; supports signed URLs/cookies, field-level encryption, and Lambda@Edge / CloudFront Functions.
A managed search service that lets you set up, manage, and scale a search solution for your website or application without operating search infrastructure.

A monitoring and observability service that collects metrics, logs, and events from AWS resources, applications, and on-premises servers. Includes CloudWatch Alarms, Dashboards, Logs Insights, Synthetics, and Contributor Insights.

An identity service that handles user sign-up, sign-in, and access control. Cognito User Pools provide a directory and authentication for application users; Identity Pools grant temporary AWS credentials to authenticated and guest users.

A natural-language processing (NLP) service that uses machine learning to extract insights and relationships from unstructured text, including entity recognition, sentiment, key phrases, topics, and language detection.
An easy-to-use omnichannel cloud contact centre that helps companies deliver superior customer service at lower cost. Pay-by-the-minute pricing with no infrastructure to manage.

A fully managed, MongoDB-compatible document database service that supports JSON workloads with millisecond latency at any scale. Decouples storage and compute; replicates six copies of data across three Availability Zones.

A fully managed serverless NoSQL key-value and document database that delivers single-digit-millisecond performance at any scale. Supports on-demand and provisioned capacity, global tables, DAX caching, point-in-time recovery, and DynamoDB Streams for change-data capture.
Block-storage volumes for use with Amazon EC2 instances. Volume types (gp3, gp2, io2, io1, st1, sc1) trade off performance and cost; snapshots are stored in S3. Volumes are Availability Zone-scoped.

Amazon Elastic Compute Cloud provides resizable compute capacity in the cloud as virtual machines called instances. Purchasing options include On-Demand, Reserved Instances, Savings Plans, and Spot Instances; instance types span general purpose, compute, memory, storage, and accelerated computing families.

A fully managed container image registry that integrates with Amazon ECS, EKS, Lambda, and Docker CLI. Supports private and public repositories, image scanning, lifecycle policies, and replication across Regions.

A highly scalable container orchestration service that supports Docker containers and lets you run and scale containerized applications on AWS. Two launch types: EC2 (manage your own instances) or Fargate (serverless).

A fully managed, elastic, serverless NFS file system that grows and shrinks automatically. Mountable from multiple EC2 instances, ECS tasks, Lambda functions, and on-premises servers; supports lifecycle management to Infrequent Access and Archive tiers.
A managed Kubernetes service that runs upstream Kubernetes on AWS without you needing to install, operate, and maintain your own Kubernetes control plane. Supports EC2 and Fargate worker nodes.

A fully managed in-memory caching service supporting Redis and Memcached engines. Used to improve application response times and reduce load on backend databases via lazy loading, write-through, or session caching patterns.
A cloud big-data platform for running large-scale distributed data processing jobs using open-source frameworks such as Apache Spark, Hive, Presto, HBase, and Hadoop.

A serverless event bus service that makes it easy to connect application data from your own apps, SaaS, and AWS services via event rules. Supports schema discovery, archive and replay, and scheduled events (successor to CloudWatch Events).

Fully managed third-party file systems on AWS: Windows File Server (SMB), Lustre (HPC), NetApp ONTAP, and OpenZFS. Choose the file system that meets your application's compatibility, performance, and management requirements.

A continuous threat-detection service that monitors AWS accounts and workloads for malicious activity using machine learning and integrated threat intelligence. Analyses VPC Flow Logs, DNS logs, and CloudTrail events.
An automated vulnerability-management service that continually scans EC2 instances, container images in ECR, and Lambda functions for software vulnerabilities and unintended network exposure.

A family of services for ingesting and processing streaming data: Kinesis Data Streams (real-time custom processing with shards), Data Firehose (load streams into S3/Redshift/OpenSearch with no code), and Data Analytics (SQL over streams).
A service for building conversational interfaces (chatbots) using voice and text, powered by the same deep-learning technologies that power Amazon Alexa.
An easy-to-use cloud platform that bundles a virtual server, storage, and networking for a fixed monthly price. Designed for simple workloads such as websites, small apps, and dev/test.
A fully managed data security service that uses machine learning and pattern matching to discover, monitor, and help protect sensitive data (PII, financial data) in Amazon S3.

A managed message broker service for Apache ActiveMQ and RabbitMQ, making it easy to migrate existing applications that rely on industry-standard messaging APIs and protocols (JMS, AMQP, STOMP, MQTT, OpenWire).
A fully managed Apache Kafka service for running production workloads. Removes the operational overhead of cluster management, broker patching, and ZooKeeper coordination.

A fast, reliable, fully managed graph database service supporting both property-graph (Gremlin, openCypher) and RDF (SPARQL) query languages. Ideal for fraud detection, knowledge graphs, and recommendation engines.
A managed service for deploying, operating, and scaling OpenSearch and Elasticsearch clusters in the cloud. Includes built-in Kibana/OpenSearch Dashboards for visualisation.
A service that turns text into lifelike speech using deep learning. Supports dozens of voices across many languages.
A cloud-native, serverless business intelligence service that makes it easy to create and publish interactive dashboards. Pay-per-session pricing; ML-powered insights and natural-language Q&A.
A managed relational database service supporting six engines: Aurora, MySQL, PostgreSQL, MariaDB, Oracle, and SQL Server. Automates provisioning, patching, backups, snapshots, and Multi-AZ failover.

A fully managed petabyte-scale cloud data warehouse. Columnar storage, massively parallel processing, and Redshift Spectrum (query S3 directly). Serverless option provisions and scales capacity automatically.

A service that makes it easy to add image and video analysis to applications: object/scene/activity detection, facial analysis, face comparison, text in images, content moderation, and celebrity recognition.

A highly available and scalable cloud DNS web service. Supports public and private hosted zones, health checks, traffic-routing policies (simple, weighted, latency-based, failover, geolocation, geoproximity, multi-value), and domain registration.

Amazon Simple Storage Service offers industry-leading scalability, data availability, security, and performance for object storage. Storage classes: Standard, Intelligent-Tiering, Standard-IA, One Zone-IA, Glacier Instant/Flexible Retrieval, and Glacier Deep Archive. Features include versioning, lifecycle policies, cross-region replication, pre-signed URLs, and event notifications.

A fully managed service that provides every developer and data scientist with the ability to build, train, and deploy machine learning models at any scale. Includes built-in algorithms, hyperparameter tuning, and model hosting.
A scalable, cost-effective email-sending service for transactional and marketing emails. Supports IP/domain reputation management, DKIM signing, and inbound email processing.

A fully managed pub/sub messaging service. Producers publish to topics; subscribers (SQS queues, Lambda, HTTP/S endpoints, email, SMS, mobile push) receive messages. Supports message filtering and FIFO topics with strict ordering and deduplication.

A fully managed message queuing service that decouples and scales microservices, distributed systems, and serverless applications. Two queue types: Standard (best-effort ordering, at-least-once delivery, near-unlimited throughput) and FIFO (strict ordering, exactly-once processing).
A machine learning service that automatically extracts text, handwriting, forms, and tabular data from scanned documents.
An automatic speech-recognition service that converts audio to text. Supports real-time streaming and batch transcription across many languages.
A neural machine-translation service that delivers fast, high-quality, customisable language translation.

A logically isolated section of the AWS Cloud where you launch AWS resources in a virtual network you define. Configure IP ranges (CIDR), subnets (public/private), route tables, internet/NAT gateways, security groups, and network ACLs.
A managed, secure desktop-as-a-service (DaaS) solution that delivers persistent Windows or Linux desktops to users on demand.

An identifier issued by Amazon API Gateway used to track and control how the API is used by individual clients. Typically combined with usage plans for throttling and quotas. Not a substitute for authentication.

Amazon Resource Names uniquely identify AWS resources across the account, partition, region, and service. Format: arn:partition:service:region:account-id:resource. Used in IAM policies, cross-service references, and CLI/SDK arguments.

One or more discrete data centres with redundant power, networking, and connectivity within an AWS Region. AZs in a Region are physically separated and connected with high-bandwidth, low-latency links. Multi-AZ deployments improve fault tolerance.
A set of tools and services for building scalable mobile and web applications. Includes hosting (CDN-backed), authentication (Cognito), data (DataStore/AppSync), and CI/CD.
A fully managed service that makes it easy to deploy from source code or a container image directly to a scalable, secure web application running on AWS.

A capability of AWS Systems Manager for creating, managing, and deploying application configurations and feature flags. Supports controlled rollouts, validation, and automatic rollback on deployment errors.
A managed serverless GraphQL service that simplifies application development by letting you create flexible APIs to securely access, manipulate, and combine data from multiple sources.
A central resource for compliance reports (SOC, PCI, ISO, FedRAMP, HIPAA, etc.) and security and compliance agreements (AWS BAA) with AWS.
A fully managed service that centralises and automates data protection across AWS services (EBS, RDS, DynamoDB, EFS, FSx, Storage Gateway, EC2 AMIs, S3, and more) and on-premises VMs.

A service that lets developers, scientists, and engineers easily and efficiently run hundreds of thousands of batch computing jobs on AWS. Dynamically provisions optimal compute resources based on the volume and resource requirements of submitted jobs.

Lets you set custom budgets that alert you when your costs or usage exceed (or are forecasted to exceed) your budgeted amount. Supports cost, usage, RI utilisation, and Savings Plans coverage budgets.
An open-source software development framework for defining cloud infrastructure in code (TypeScript, JavaScript, Python, Java, Go, C#) and provisioning it through AWS CloudFormation.

A service that lets you easily provision, manage, and deploy public and private SSL/TLS certificates for use with AWS services and your internal connected resources. Public certs are free; auto-renewed.
Guidance and best practices, organised into six perspectives (Business, People, Governance, Platform, Security, Operations), for accelerating an organisation's cloud journey.
A cloud-based integrated development environment (IDE) that lets you write, run, and debug code with just a browser. Pre-packaged with the AWS CLI and common runtimes.

An infrastructure-as-code service that provisions and manages AWS and third-party resources by treating infrastructure as JSON or YAML templates. Supports stacks, change sets, drift detection, nested stacks, StackSets, and DeletionPolicy/UpdateReplacePolicy.
Cloud-based hardware security modules (HSMs) that let you generate and use your own encryption keys in dedicated, FIPS 140-2 Level 3 validated hardware.
A browser-based shell pre-authenticated with your AWS Management Console credentials. Comes pre-installed with AWS CLI, common shells (bash, PowerShell, zsh), and runtimes.
A service that enables governance, compliance, and operational/risk auditing of an AWS account. Logs API calls across services into trails delivered to S3, CloudWatch Logs, and EventBridge.
A fully managed software artifact repository that supports popular package managers (npm, PyPI, Maven, NuGet, etc.) and integrates with public package repositories via upstream connections.
A fully managed continuous-integration service that compiles source code, runs tests, and produces software packages ready to deploy. Build instructions live in a buildspec.yml file.
A fully managed source-control service that hosts secure private Git repositories. Integrates with IAM for access control and triggers (CloudWatch Events / Lambda) on repository events.
A deployment service that automates application deployments to EC2, on-premises instances, Lambda, or ECS. Supports in-place, blue/green, canary, and linear deployments via an appspec.yml file.

A continuous-delivery service that automates release pipelines for fast and reliable application and infrastructure updates. Models source -> build -> test -> deploy stages with manual approval actions and parallel execution.

A service that continuously monitors and records AWS resource configurations and lets you automate the evaluation of recorded configurations against desired configurations (Config Rules + auto-remediation).

The easiest way to set up and govern a secure, multi-account AWS environment (a landing zone). Provides guardrails (preventive SCPs + detective AWS Config rules) and account-vending via Service Catalog.
A free tool for viewing and analysing AWS costs and usage over time. Includes filters, grouping, forecasts, RI/Savings Plans recommendations, and Rightsizing Recommendations.

A dedicated, private network connection from on-premises to AWS. Provides consistent network performance, lower bandwidth costs at scale, and the ability to extend on-premises networks into AWS through VLANs.

An easy-to-use service for deploying and scaling web applications and services developed with Java, .NET, PHP, Node.js, Python, Ruby, Go, and Docker. You retain full control of the underlying AWS resources; configure via .ebextensions.
A serverless compute engine for containers that works with Amazon ECS and EKS. You don't manage EC2 instances; pay only for the CPU and memory your tasks/pods consume.

A security management service that lets you centrally configure and manage firewall rules (WAF, Shield Advanced, Network Firewall, security groups, Route 53 Resolver DNS Firewall) across multiple accounts and resources.
A networking service that improves the availability and performance of applications by routing traffic through the AWS global network and providing static anycast IP addresses as a fixed entry point.

Provides a personalised view of the performance and availability of AWS services that you are using, plus alerts about scheduled changes and outages that affect your resources (Personal Health) alongside global service health (Service Health).

A service for securely controlling access to AWS resources. Manages users, groups, roles, identity providers, and policies (identity-based, resource-based, permission boundaries, SCPs). Apply the principle of least privilege and protect the root user with MFA.

Successor to AWS Single Sign-On. Centrally manages workforce access to multiple AWS accounts and business applications. Supports SAML 2.0 IdPs and built-in identity store; assigns permission sets to users and groups.

A managed service for creating and controlling cryptographic keys used to encrypt data. Supports customer-managed keys (CMKs), AWS-managed keys, key rotation, key policies, grants, and envelope encryption via data keys.

A serverless compute service that runs code in response to events (API Gateway, S3, DynamoDB Streams, EventBridge, SQS, etc.) without provisioning or managing servers. Pay only for compute time consumed; supports up to 10 GB of memory, 15-min timeout, layers, aliases/versions, and provisioned concurrency.
A service that streamlines managing software licenses (Microsoft, SAP, Oracle, IBM, etc.) from vendors across AWS and on-premises environments.
A curated digital catalog that customers can use to find, buy, deploy, and manage third-party software, data, and services. Charges roll up into the customer's AWS bill.
A single location to discover, plan, and track migrations across multiple AWS and partner solutions.
A managed network firewall service for VPCs. Provides stateful inspection, intrusion prevention (Suricata-compatible rules), and protocol filtering.
A service for centrally managing and governing multiple AWS accounts. Provides consolidated billing, organizational units (OUs), Service Control Policies (SCPs), and resource sharing across accounts.
Fully managed racks (or smaller 1U/2U servers) of AWS-designed infrastructure that bring AWS services and operating model to virtually any on-premises or edge location.
A web-based planning tool you can use to create cost estimates for AWS services. Supports modelling multi-service architectures and exporting estimates as CSV/PDF.
A service that helps you securely share AWS resources (Transit Gateways, subnets, License Manager configurations, Route 53 Resolver rules, etc.) with any AWS account or through AWS Organizations.
A service to manage, retrieve, and rotate database credentials, API keys, and other secrets throughout their lifecycle. Native rotation for RDS, Redshift, DocumentDB; custom rotation via Lambda.

A cloud security posture management (CSPM) service that aggregates, organises, and prioritises security findings from AWS services (GuardDuty, Inspector, Macie, IAM Access Analyzer) and third-party tools against industry standards (CIS, PCI DSS, AWS Foundational Security Best Practices).

An open-source framework for building serverless applications. SAM templates extend CloudFormation with shorthand syntax for Lambda functions, APIs, and DynamoDB tables. The SAM CLI provides local testing (sam local invoke), guided deploy, and packaging.

Lets organisations create and manage catalogs of IT services (built as CloudFormation products) that are approved for use on AWS. Users browse and self-provision products under governed configurations.

A managed Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) protection service. Shield Standard (free, automatically on for all customers) defends against common network/transport attacks; Shield Advanced (paid) adds proactive engagement with the AWS DDoS Response Team and cost-protection.
Physical devices for petabyte- and exabyte-scale data transfer, edge computing, and storage in environments with limited connectivity. Snowcone (8 TB), Snowball Edge (80 TB), Snowmobile (100 PB).

A serverless workflow service that lets you coordinate multiple AWS services into serverless applications. Define state machines in Amazon States Language (JSON) with Standard (long-running, exactly-once) or Express (high-volume, at-least-once) workflows.
A hybrid cloud storage service that connects on-premises environments to virtually unlimited cloud storage. Gateway types: S3 File, FSx File, Volume (cached/stored), and Tape.

Four tiers: Basic (free, account & billing), Developer (business-hours email, one primary contact), Business (24/7 phone/chat/email, all customers, third-party software guidance), and Enterprise On-Ramp / Enterprise (24/7 with 15-min response for critical issues, designated Technical Account Manager, Infrastructure Event Management, Concierge support team).

An operations hub that provides operational data and automated actions across AWS resources. Includes Parameter Store (config + secrets), Session Manager (shell access without SSH), Patch Manager, Run Command, Inventory, OpsCenter, and Automation.
Fully managed support for file transfers in and out of Amazon S3 and Amazon EFS over SFTP, FTPS, FTP, and AS2 protocols.

An online tool that provides real-time guidance to help you provision resources following AWS best practices across five categories: cost optimisation, performance, security, fault tolerance, and service limits.

A web-application firewall that protects web applications and APIs against common exploits (SQL injection, XSS, bots) by inspecting HTTP/S requests. Deployable on CloudFront, ALB, API Gateway, AppSync, and Cognito user pools.

A distributed-tracing service that helps developers analyse and debug production, distributed applications. Visualises requests as a service map; segments and subsegments expose latency and errors per call.

A deployment strategy that runs two identical production environments (blue = current, green = new). Traffic is shifted from blue to green; if issues arise you can instantly roll back by switching traffic back to blue. Native support in CodeDeploy, Elastic Beanstalk, and Lambda alias routing.

A deployment strategy where the new version is rolled out to a small subset of users/instances first. If metrics look good, the rest are migrated. CodeDeploy and API Gateway support canary traffic shifting natively.
Classless Inter-Domain Routing notation for IP address ranges (e.g. 10.0.0.0/16). VPCs and subnets are defined with CIDR blocks; subnet CIDRs must be within and not overlap inside the VPC CIDR.

The latency penalty incurred when AWS Lambda must initialise a new execution environment (download code, start runtime, run init code) before invoking the handler. Mitigated by provisioned concurrency, keeping function packages small, and lazy-loading SDK clients outside the handler.

Amazon DynamoDB Accelerator is a fully managed, write-through, in-memory cache for DynamoDB that delivers microsecond response times for read-heavy workloads. Sits between the app and the DynamoDB table; uses the same DynamoDB API.

Sites that CloudFront uses to cache copies of content for faster delivery to users in any location. There are many more edge locations than Regions, distributed worldwide; also used by Route 53, Global Accelerator, and Shield.

Automatically distributes incoming traffic across multiple targets and Availability Zones. ALB (Layer 7, HTTP/HTTPS, host/path/header rules), NLB (Layer 4, TCP/UDP/TLS, ultra-low latency, static IPs), GWLB (Layer 3 transparent for security appliances), and Classic Load Balancer (legacy).

Protection of data while it is stored on disk. AWS services integrate with KMS for server-side encryption (SSE-KMS, SSE-S3, SSE-C in S3; KMS-encrypted EBS volumes, RDS instances, DynamoDB tables, etc.).

Protection of data while it moves between systems. Implemented with TLS/SSL between clients and AWS endpoints, ACM-managed certificates on load balancers and CloudFront, and VPC peering / VPN / Direct Connect MACsec for inter-network traffic.

A pattern where a customer master key (CMK / KMS key) encrypts a data key, and the data key encrypts the data itself. The encrypted data key is stored alongside the ciphertext; this avoids sending large amounts of data to KMS.

A DynamoDB index with a partition key, optional sort key, and projection that can be different from the base table. Supports eventually consistent reads across all partitions; creatable/deletable at any time.

A property where multiple invocations of the same operation produce the same result. Critical for retry-safe APIs; achieve via deduplication keys (SQS FIFO MessageDeduplicationId, S3 multipart uploads, DynamoDB conditional writes, idempotency tokens in SDK requests).

Practice of managing and provisioning infrastructure through machine-readable definition files rather than manual processes. On AWS: CloudFormation, AWS SAM, AWS CDK, and third-party tools like Terraform/Pulumi.

A horizontally scaled, redundant, highly available VPC component that allows communication between resources in a VPC and the public internet. Required for resources in public subnets to be reachable.
A compact, URL-safe means of representing claims between two parties as a signed JSON object. Commonly issued by Amazon Cognito user pools and consumed by API Gateway authorizers.

A DynamoDB index sharing the same partition key as the base table but with a different sort key. Strongly or eventually consistent reads; must be created at table-creation time and limited to 5 per table.

An extra layer of protection on top of username and password, requiring a second factor (virtual or hardware MFA device, FIDO security key) for sign-in. Strongly recommended for the root user and all privileged IAM identities.

A managed network address translation service that lets resources in a private subnet initiate outbound connections to the internet (or other AWS services) while remaining unreachable from the internet.

A time-limited URL generated by an authorised principal that grants temporary access to an S3 object (GET or PUT) without requiring the requester to have AWS credentials. Common for browser uploads/downloads.
A Lambda feature that initialises a requested number of execution environments and keeps them warm. Eliminates cold starts for predictable workloads. Charged per GB-second of provisioned capacity.
A Lambda configuration that guarantees the maximum number of concurrent executions for a function (and caps it). Prevents one function from consuming all account-level unreserved concurrency.

A physical location in the world where AWS clusters data centres. Each Region consists of multiple, isolated, and physically separate Availability Zones. Customers choose Regions for latency, compliance/data residency, service availability, and cost.

A billing discount applied to EC2 (and a few other services) in exchange for a 1- or 3-year commitment. Up to ~72% off On-Demand prices; Standard (locked attributes) or Convertible (can change instance family). Largely superseded by Savings Plans.

A flexible pricing model offering lower compute prices in exchange for a commitment to a consistent usage amount ($/hour) for 1 or 3 years. Compute Savings Plans (most flexible, EC2/Fargate/Lambda); EC2 Instance Savings Plans (instance-family-specific); SageMaker Savings Plans.

Language-specific libraries that simplify calling AWS APIs from your application. Officially supported SDKs: Python (boto3), JavaScript/TypeScript, Java, Go, .NET, Ruby, PHP, C++, Kotlin, Rust, Swift.

A virtual stateful firewall attached to an EC2 instance / ENI. Allow rules only (no deny rules); return traffic for established connections is automatically permitted. Operates at the instance level (unlike Network ACLs at subnet level).

An AWS Organizations policy type that sets the maximum permissions for accounts in the organisation. SCPs don't grant permissions but act as a guardrail filter over IAM policies. Attached to roots, OUs, or accounts.

AWS's framework for security responsibilities: AWS is responsible for security OF the cloud (hardware, hypervisor, managed services); the customer is responsible for security IN the cloud (data, IAM, OS patching for unmanaged services, application code, network/firewall config).

Spare EC2 capacity at up to 90% off On-Demand prices. AWS can reclaim the instance with a 2-minute notification. Suitable for fault-tolerant, flexible workloads (batch jobs, CI/CD, big data, stateless web).

AWS Security Token Service grants temporary, limited-privilege credentials for IAM principals or federated users via AssumeRole, AssumeRoleWithSAML, AssumeRoleWithWebIdentity, GetSessionToken, and GetFederationToken APIs.
Key-value pairs you assign to AWS resources for metadata, organisation, automation, IAM access control (ABAC), and cost allocation (activate as Cost Allocation Tags in Billing).

A network transit hub that connects VPCs, on-premises networks (via Direct Connect or VPN), and other Transit Gateways in a hub-and-spoke topology. Simplifies what would otherwise be a full mesh of VPC peering connections.

An SQS configuration that hides a message from other consumers after a worker receives it, giving the worker time to process and delete it. If the worker fails to delete before the timeout expires, the message becomes visible again.
AWS Site-to-Site VPN creates encrypted IPsec tunnels between an on-premises network and a VPC. AWS Client VPN provides per-user OpenVPN access to AWS and on-premises networks.

AWS's set of best practices organised into six pillars: Operational Excellence, Security, Reliability, Performance Efficiency, Cost Optimization, and Sustainability. The Well-Architected Tool reviews workloads against these pillars.
